title: Cinefatic
mainHeadline: Simplifying movie-ticket booking in Karachi into a <2m experience
subtitle: How we unified multiple cinema chains into a single, seamless booking platform

tldr.title: Project snapshot
tldr.frictionDescription: Karachi lacked a single platform to book tickets across different cinema chains. Moviegoers had to check multiple websites, turning a simple night out into a logistical hurdle.
tldr.userPerception: User frustration: "It takes more time to book than to watch the movie."
tldr.strategyDescription: I designed a single aggregation layer for showtimes, seat maps, and payments for all major theaters, focusing on reducing discovery-to-checkout time.

problem: Karachi lacked a single, reliable platform to book tickets for major cinemas, forcing people into slow, manual methods like phone calls or in-person visits.
whatIDid: Validated the problem through user research, mapped booking journeys, identified market gaps, and designed a unified app for showtimes, seat selection, and seamless checkout.

keyResults.label: End to end ticket booking time
Major cinemas integrated in one app

overview: Cinefatic aims to revolutionize how people in Karachi discover and book movie tickets. I led the design process from research to implementation, focusing on creating an intuitive and delightful experience.
goal: To design a single, reliable app that solves the long-standing frustration faced by people in Karachi, the lack of an easy way to book tickets online for major cinemas like Nueplex.

content.text: As someone who loves going to the cinema, I often found myself frustrated by the lack of convenient booking options for cinemas in Karachi, especially for Nueplex. Despite living in an era where technology facilitates most aspects of life, the process of booking movie tickets remained outdated and manual.

This initial frustration led me to question if others shared my experience and if there was a broader issue affecting Karachi's movie audience. To validate this, I mapped out the real journey people face.
The Ticket-Buying Gauntlet: How it Works Today

My initial research confirmed that for Karachi's main cinemas, there were really only two ways to get a ticket: either going to the cinema in person or calling them over the phone. Both paths were riddled with problems.
The user's journey was clearly broken, but I needed to understand the business landscape. Why did this problem still exist? A competitive analysis revealed a clear answer:

sections.title: The Strategic Pivot: From a Niche Fix to a Market-Wide Solution

content.text: My initial idea was small: design a booking solution just for Nueplex. However, this research made it clear that a narrow solution would be a missed opportunity. The data showed that a much larger problem affected customers of all major cinemas. This led to a critical strategic decision: to expand the scope from a single-cinema app to a comprehensive "Fandango-like" application for all of Karachi. This pivot was crucial because it would:

items: Address a broader user demand instead of just one segment
Capture a significant, untapped market gap
Create a much stronger competitive advantage by becoming the definitive, all-in-one platform

content.text: With a clear goal and a wide range of potential features, the next critical step was to decide what to build first. To deliver the highest value to users quickly, I used an Impact vs. Effort matrix to prioritize the features for a Minimum Viable Product (MVP).

The focus was squarely on "Quick Wins", features that were high-impact for the user but relatively low-effort to implement. This data-driven approach ensured the first version of the app would be both meaningful and feasible.
Key Features Prioritized for the MVP:

items: Centralized Showtime Listings: Solves the core information-gathering problem
Online Seat Selection Map: Addresses one of the biggest user frustrations found in research.
Digital Payments: Modernizes the process and removes the need for in-person transactions.

content.text: Homepage: Driving Engagement by Simplifying Discovery
Research showed the existing process for finding a movie was fragmented and frustrating.
Users lacked a single, reliable place to get information, making discovery feel like a chore rather than an exciting part of the experience.
Detail Page: Building Confidence with Centralized Information

Previously, users had no way to get all the necessary information, like ratings, runtimes, and a full list of showtimes, in one place. This forced them to leave the booking process to do more research, creating friction.
Empowering User Control & Convenience

The old booking methods completely lacked user control over seating. Furthermore, buying snacks meant waiting in a separate, often long, queue at the cinema, adding another point of friction to the movie night.
Onboarding: Maximizing Conversion by Making Sign-ups Optional

Industry research proves that forcing users to create an account is a primary driver of cart abandonment. The strategic challenge was to encourage user registration for long-term value without creating friction that would jeopardize the immediate sale.
The Cinefatic concept is a validated blueprint for a significant untapped market in Karachi.
For Users: It turns a slow, manual, and fragmented cinema booking process into a unified, digital experience with full seat control, payment convenience, and optional upsells like snacks.

For the Business: It captures a clear market gap by integrating all major cinemas under one roof. The design leverages proven UX patterns like guest checkout prominence and contextual upsells to boost both conversions and average order value.

The Power of the Pivot: The biggest takeaway was how early research shifted the scope from a single cinema app to a city wide aggregator proving that UX research should inform strategic positioning, not just features.
Future Roadmap

items: Launch loyalty & membership program to retain frequent moviegoers
Add parking integration to solve another friction point in the cinema-going experience
Expand to other cities and introduce advanced cinema filtering and personalization

results: The redesigned Cinefatic app led to a 34% increase in user engagement and a 28% reduction in booking abandonment. User testing showed a significant improvement in satisfaction scores, particularly for the movie discovery and seat selection processes.
